title: We are ALL Kings, That's the thing about this Day in Age. Jesus would say so

transcript.text: Hi, this is a video that I wanted to do recently, and it's called We Are All Kings.
I think that's the main thing about the world these days, the thing to realize, and the thing to actually become, is that we are all kings.
And it's a logical thing to think about, a logical thing to happen to humanity, if you look at the trajectory.
That's why studying history is really important, because if you study history and you know where we came from, then therefore you can know where we're going, obviously.

If you're looking at a sea and you're seeing a drifting, floating thing on it, and you knew where it was five minutes ago, well then you just draw the line out and extend it out, and you're going to know where it's going to be in the next five years.
It's the simplest of concepts.
So, back in the past, normal people didn't mean shit, didn't know shit, didn't have shit, didn't have control of shit.
Serfs, serfdom, slavery.
People would have to get permission from their master, the lord of the manor, or whatever, the king, the duke, just to move to the next valley over.
And they'd work all day long, six days a week, and yeah, sure, maybe they had some land of their own, but they were only allowed to toil it like half of a day out of a week.
And the rest of the time it was their master lord, and if their master lord said they had to go to war, well, they had to go to war.
So, you know, and then you look at the king, and the king basically owns everything.
Like, everything he owns, he can go ride his horse into the chalet and be like, that house, let's stay there tonight.
And everybody's bowing down to him.
Everybody within sight does everything for the king.
They come, even in America, you know, you had the king's territory.
They'd come and be like, get off the king's grass.
This is king's territory.
So, it was like absolute control, absolute power for like one guy, for the whole country, versus everybody else to have zero power, zero control, zero nothing.
But then as time goes on, people get more and more and more power, gradually.
Serfdom ends, and then people get their own land, and then they'd be able to travel around, and then absolute monarchies turn into constitutional monarchies.
And then, and then we have like modern day here, where people get to go over where they want.

And, and then the wealth discrepancy changes, like from primitive countries and societies to more advanced ones, where, where the minimum wage dude is making a much larger percentage of the, of the pie as, as the richest dude in the world, in the, in the country.
Or the, you know, the general last, bottom 20th percent are working, um, are, are making a more bigger piece of the pie than the highest 20th percent of the people.
So, what do you do?
You just carry that trajectory out into the future, just like if you're looking at the little floating cork.
And what does that mean?
It means in the future, we will have this one vision that everybody hopes on, and where everybody's a king, everybody's equal.
